articles.title: 'Odisha ki garmi': Man tries to cook omelette in 44 degrees amid ongoing heatwave, leaves netizens shocked
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Odisha
articles.full_text:
India is currently going through an intense heatwave that has people hiding up in their abodes, experimenting with movies and trying online trends. Typically, netizens of other countries try experimenting with making desserts or heating up water in extreme cold and hot weather.
Now, a man's unusual experiment in the intense summer heat of
Odisha has caught the attention of social media users. The post was shared on Instagram by user @life.is.foodie_ with the caption reading "Sambalpur at 44 degrees." In the clip, the man can be seen standing on his terrace during the peak afternoon heat. He places a pan under direct sunlight, pours oil into it and gestures towards the sun, highlighting the intensity of the heat.
He then cracks open two eggs into a glass, whisks them and pours the mixture into the pan. Within seconds, the edges of the omelette begin to fluff up, indicating it is cooking under the heat. “Odisha ki garmi,” says the text in the video.
Social media reactions
The man's video invited mixed reactions online. While some users reacted to the video with amazement and humour, others were sceptical of the setup.
"Hp Gas ki tension khatam," quipped a user online.
“Omelette is okay, I was thinking you might get fried,” said one, while another added. “Kolkata mein omelette overcooked ho jaata.”
Some were suspicious about the intensity of the heat, enough to cook an omelette, writing: "Tell me honestly is it not a preheated tawa?" and writing, "I think the pan was already heated before making the omelette."
Others called the experiment practical and innovative, with remarks such as “Good use of solar energy.”
Heatwave in India
The summer of 2026 has seen temperatures soar sky high, even past 45 degrees Celsius in many parts of the country. Extreme conditions have been gripping northern, central, and western states, with severe heat alerts in places like Delhi-NCR, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, and Rajasthan.
As many as 8,056 excess deaths can occur during a five-day intense heatwave in Uttar Pradesh, according to a study published on May 26, making it India’s most vulnerable state for heat-related fatalities. Moreover, with the nights being equally warm, the little window of relief has also disappeared, leaving behind agitated and unwell citizens.
In this situation, videos like these not only serve as an alarm over the rising national and global temperatures but also remind people of just how much such high temperatures have been normalised over the years.

articles.title: Man Uses Odisha Heatwave To Cook Eggs In His Terrace; Netizens: “Tell Me Tawa Is Not Preheated” - curlytales.com
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Odisha is currently experiencing intense heatwave conditions along with hot and humid weather. A man in Sambalpur recently showed how hot it is outside by trying to cook eggs on a frying pan on his balcony, and you won’t believe it, the eggs started cooking!
Odisha Man Tries Cooking Eggs Under The Sun
View this post on Instagram
The intense heatwave-like conditions have caught social media’s attention, but in an unusual way. A man in Sambalpur, Odisha, recently tried to cook eggs under direct sunlight on his balcony, and the clip is now going viral online. While many people believed the heat was strong, others even called it a setup.
The post was shared by @life.is.foodie_. In the video, the man pours oil onto a tawa and places it under direct sunlight. After that, he cracks open two eggs into a glass, whisks them, and pours the mixture onto the heated pan, just like an omelette. Just a few seconds later, the edges of the egg start to cook and fluff up, seemingly proving just how hot it is in Odisha. The man also added a caption that read, “Sambalpur At 44°.”

Heatwave Or Just A Creative Idea?
The video is going viral. While the man tried to show the intensity of the heatwave in Odisha, some people were still doubtful. One user asked, “Tell me honestly, is it not a preheated tawa? Another user commented, “Kolkata mein omelette overcooked ho jata.”
Some comments were quite humorous, too. A few people suggested that the man visit Assam, where some people are still using blankets at night. Another Instagram user sarcastically asked him to cook biryani outside. We do not know whether the eggs were cooked because of the heat or whether the tawa had been preheated. The only thing we know is that the idea behind the viral video was certainly creative!

Have you ever tried cooking eggs under direct sunlight?
Cover Image Courtesy: @life.is.foodie_/X

Many parts of Odisha are currently experiencing hot, humid, and heatwave-like conditions.

articles.title: Odisha Man Cooks Omelette Under Sun Amid Extreme Heatwave? Viral Video Sparks Buzz - Times Now
articles.full_text:
Sambalpur, Odisha: As Odisha grapples with a relentless heatwave, a viral social media video has sparked widespread discussion by appearing to show an omelette being cooked under nothing but the scorching afternoon sun.
Omelette Under the Sun? Viral Sambalpur Video Sparks Debate as Odisha Sizzles at 44°C (Image Source - Social Media)
The clip, which has been circulating rapidly across Instagram and other platforms, was reportedly filmed in Sambalpur, a city that has been recording temperatures above 44 degrees Celsius in recent days. The unusual demonstration has caught the attention of thousands online, with many citing it as evidence of the intense heat gripping parts of the state, while others remain unconvinced about whether sunlight alone was responsible for cooking the eggs.
What are common hygiene concerns with street food in India?
How do extreme heat conditions affect food safety?
What traditional remedies are suggested for heat protection?
The video was shared by Instagram user @life.is.foodie_ with the caption, "Sambalpur At 44 Degrees." In the footage, a man stands on a terrace during peak daytime hours and places a frying pan directly under the blazing sun. He then pours oil into the pan and gestures towards the clear sky, emphasizing the extreme weather conditions.
Shortly afterwards, he cracks two eggs into a glass container, whisks them, and pours the mixture into the pan. Within moments, portions of the egg appear to solidify, with the edges visibly lifting and taking on the appearance of a cooking omelette. The video also displays the text "Odisha Ki Garmi," underscoring the severe heat being experienced across the region.
The footage quickly generated a flood of reactions online. Many viewers expressed astonishment, saying the clip highlighted just how unbearable summer conditions have become in parts of Odisha. Others, however, questioned the authenticity of the demonstration, suggesting that the pan may have been preheated beforehand or that additional heat sources could have been involved off-camera.
Several users pointed out that while metal surfaces exposed to direct sunlight can become extremely hot during intense heatwaves, fully cooking an omelette solely through ambient temperatures and sunlight would generally require highly specific conditions. This debate has only added to the video's popularity, with users continuing to discuss and dissect the footage across social media platforms.
Regardless of whether the experiment was entirely solar-powered, the video has succeeded in drawing attention to the harsh weather conditions currently affecting Odisha. The state has witnessed soaring temperatures in recent weeks, prompting authorities and health experts to issue advisories urging residents to stay indoors during peak afternoon hours, remain hydrated, and take precautions against heat-related illnesses.
As the conversation continues online, the viral omelette clip has become one of the latest examples of how social media users are documenting and reacting to India's extreme summer temperatures, turning an ordinary cooking experiment into a nationwide talking point.
